description_long: This object consists of three distinct grave markers arranged side by side. The central marker is a prominent stepped pedestal topped with a large Latin cross, giving it a monumental appearance. To the left, there is a traditional arched headstone with a simple rectangular base, while to the right, a smaller rectangular base supports a Latin cross with a slightly tilted orientation. All markers appear to be made of solid stone, likely granite or concrete, with a uniform dark gray coloration and matte finish. The surfaces are smooth but not polished, suggesting weather resistance and durability. No visible inscriptions or decorative motifs are present on any of the markers.
description: A set of three dark gray stone grave markers, including two crosses and one arched headstone, all with smooth matte surfaces.
description_view_0: A side view showing the narrow profiles of the three grave markers, with the central cross and stepped base most prominent.
description_view_1: A frontal view displaying all three grave markers: a central stepped cross, an arched headstone on the left, and a smaller cross on the right.
description_view_2: A rear side view highlighting the stepped structure of the central cross and the backs of the other two markers.
description_view_3: A mirrored frontal view with the arched headstone now on the right and the smaller cross on the left, central cross in the middle.
